title: Purpose of Financial Markets
objective: Understand the historical role of liquidity and value exchange.
content:
# The Purpose of Markets

Financial markets exist to facilitate the exchange of risk and value. Understanding this is the first step to thinking like an institution.
forexAdaptation:
## Fiat Reserve System
Central Banks manage currency valuation to maintain trade balance. We trade the 'Yield Differential'.
cryptoAdaptation:
## Web3 Economy
Crypto exists to remove the middleman. We trade the 'On-Chain Inflow' metrics.
goldAdaptation:
## Hard Money Heritage
Gold is the only asset with zero counterparty risk. We trade it as the 'Inflation Compass'.

options.text: Global capital is seeking the higher yield available in this currency.
options.rationale: The Yield Differential is the single most powerful macro driver in the FX market.

options.text: Traders are excited and buying the news.
options.feedback: Emotional 'excitement' doesn't move trillions. Yield differentials do.
options.rationale: Retail sentiment is a symptom, not the cause.

interactiveTaskData.contextPrompt: A Central Bank announces an unexpected 50bps rate hike. The currency pair immediate rallies 100 pips. Why did this happen in the context of global liquidity?

title: The Zero-Sum Fallacy
objective: Deconstruct the myth of retail vs. institutional 'winning'.
content:
# Zero-Sum vs Liquidity Transfer

You are not playing against a chart; you are playing against liquidity algorithms.

title: The Probability Matrix
objective: Thinking in bets. The Casino Analogy.
content:
# The Probability Matrix

Like a casino, we have a small edge. We don't care if we lose a single hand; we care about the outcome over 1000 hands.

title: Supply & Demand Fundamentals
objective: Basic economics of value and scarcity.
content:
# Supply & Demand

The fundamental truth of value. If something is rare and desired, price rises.

title: Inflation Mechanics
objective: How fiat debasement drives asset pricing.
content:
# Inflation Mechanics

Why $100 today buys less than $100 ten years ago.

title: Central Banking Basics
objective: The role of the Fed, ECB, and BOJ.
content:
# Central Banking

The ultimate controllers of liquidity.

Why traders double down after a loss.

title: Loss Aversion
objective: Why losing hurts more than winning feels good.
content:

Cutting winners early and letting losers run.

title: R-Multiples
objective: Thinking in risk units, not dollars.
content:

Stop thinking in dollars. Start thinking in 'R'.

title: Win Rate vs RR
content:
# Win Rate vs Risk/Reward

A trader with a 35% win rate can be highly profitable.

title: Asymmetry of Drawdown
content:
# Asymmetry of Drawdown

If you lose 50%, you need a 100% gain to breakeven.
